steps.title: Identify the cylinder volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder is $$V = \pi r^2 h$$ where $r$ is the radius and $h$ is the height. We are given $r = 14$ m and $h = 12$ m.

steps.title: Substitute values and compute
steps.content:
Substitute into the formula: $$V = \pi (14)^2 (12) = \pi \cdot 196 \cdot 12$$
Compute $196 \times 12 = 2,352$

Therefore, $V = 2,352\pi$ m³. That matches choice D — D is correct.
